title: Advanced Passive — Reports and Abstracts
description: Reporting structures with IT IS SAID / BELIEVED / THOUGHT, and double passive constructions.
summary_pt_br: Passivas impessoais: 'It is said that...' / 'He is said to be...'. Acadêmico, jornalístico, formal.

scenes.subtitle_en: Reporting structures with IT IS SAID / BELIEVED / THOUGHT, and double passive constructions.
scenes.subtitle_pt: Passivas impessoais: 'It is said that...' / 'He is said to be...'. Acadêmico, jornalístico, formal.

scenes.text_en: Reporting structures with IT IS SAID / BELIEVED / THOUGHT, and double passive constructions. Today's focus: advanced passive — reports and abstracts. It is believed that the artifact is over a thousand years old.
scenes.text_pt: Passivas impessoais: 'It is said that...' / 'He is said to be...'. Acadêmico, jornalístico, formal. Foco de hoje: advanced passive — reports and abstracts. Acredita-se que o artefato tem mais de mil anos.

scenes.title: Advanced Passive — Reports and Abstracts
scenes.body_en: IT + BE + past participle + THAT... : 'It is believed that cats have nine lives.' Or: 'Cats are believed to have nine lives.' Used in academic, news, and formal writing.
scenes.body_pt: Passivas impessoais: 'It is said that...' / 'He is said to be...'. Acadêmico, jornalístico, formal.

examples.en: It is believed that the artifact is over a thousand years old.
examples.pt: Acredita-se que o artefato tem mais de mil anos.

examples.en: The CEO is said to be considering a merger.
examples.pt: Diz-se que o CEO está considerando uma fusão.

examples.en: The study is expected to be published next year.
examples.pt: Espera-se que o estudo seja publicado ano que vem.

scenes.title: Key vocabulary — Advanced Passive — Reports and Abstracts

exercise.question: Complete: "It is ___ that the artifact is over a thousand years old."
exercise.explanation: Full sentence: "It is believed that the artifact is over a thousand years old."
exercise.hint_pt_br: Acredita-se que o artefato tem mais de mil anos.

exercise.question: Which sentence is correct?
options: I haven't heard anything about that.
The CEO is said to be considering a merger.
exercise.explanation: The correct sentence is: "The CEO is said to be considering a merger."
exercise.hint_pt_br: Diz-se que o CEO está considerando uma fusão.

exercise.explanation: Five key terms from the lesson. Match each English word to its Portuguese meaning.

scenes.title: Advanced Passive — Reports and Abstracts — dialog
scenes.pt_summary: Diálogo sobre advanced passive — reports and abstracts. Pratique as frases marcadas.

turns.speaker: ai
turns.text: Let's practice impersonal reporting. 'People say the mayor will resign.'

turns.speaker: user
turns.target: The mayor is said to be resigning.

turns.speaker: ai
turns.text: Try another. 'Experts think AI will change education.'

turns.speaker: user
turns.target: AI is thought to be changing education.

turns.speaker: ai
turns.text: Use IT structure. 'We believe the policy is unfair.'

turns.speaker: user
turns.target: It is believed that the policy is unfair.

turns.speaker: ai
turns.text: What about past reporting? 'People knew he was sick.'

turns.speaker: user
turns.target: He was known to be sick at the time.

turns.speaker: user
turns.target: The suspect is alleged to have fled abroad.

turns.speaker: ai
turns.text: Impersonal passives turn opinion into register.

scenes.title: Kumar's writing seminar
scenes.pt_summary: Ouça com atenção. Depois, escreva em inglês o que entendeu — resumo, detalhes e sua opinião. O professor vai ler e comentar.
scenes.prompt_en: Summarize three rules Kumar shared. Use at least two impersonal passive constructions.
scenes.prompt_pt: Escreva em inglês o que ouviu. Resumo curto com quem falou, o que disseram e sua opinião.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.en: In Kumar's Saturday writing seminar, students learned how passives shape the voice of serious writing.

paragraphs.speaker: Kumar
paragraphs.en: It is believed that sentences feel more formal when the speaker disappears behind the words.

paragraphs.speaker: Student
paragraphs.en: So news writers use these to avoid sounding personal or emotional?

paragraphs.speaker: Kumar
paragraphs.en: Exactly. The mayor is said to be considering resignation — no one person owns that claim.

paragraphs.speaker: Student
paragraphs.en: These structures are known to be common in academic papers too, right?

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.en: Register in English, Kumar taught, is not about length — it's about who you let speak through your sentence.

scenes.title: Recap reading
scenes.passage_en: At Amazing School, the class just finished "Advanced Passive — Reports and Abstracts". Reporting structures with IT IS SAID / BELIEVED / THOUGHT, and double passive constructions. It is believed that the artifact is over a thousand years old. The CEO is said to be considering a merger. Mr. Kumar reminded the students that practice is the key — listen, read aloud, write your own examples. Every small step adds up.

check.question: According to Mr. Kumar, what is the key to learning?
check.explanation: Practice — listen, read aloud, write your own examples.
check.hint_pt_br: Prática: ouvir, ler em voz alta, escrever exemplos.

scenes.title: Listen to Bia
scenes.audio_text_en: Hello! I'm Bia, from Praia do Sol. Today in class we studied "Advanced Passive — Reports and Abstracts". I liked it because it was clear and useful. See you next time!

check.question: What did Bia study today?
options: A grammar rule from last year
check.explanation: She says: "Advanced Passive — Reports and Abstracts".
check.hint_pt_br: Ela fala o título: "Advanced Passive — Reports and Abstracts".

scenes.title: Go deeper — free resources
scenes.body_pt: Fontes gratuitas e confiáveis para continuar praticando. Salve os links e volte sempre.
